features.summary: Alphabet reported Q2 2022 revenue of $69.7B (up 13% YoY), with strong performance in Search ($40.7B, up 14%) driven by travel and retail, while YouTube ads grew modestly at 5% due to tough comparisons and advertiser pullbacks. Google Cloud reached $6.3B (up 36%), surpassing $6B quarterly revenue for the first time. The company announced plans to slow hiring pace and sharpen focus on critical priorities like AI, Search, and Cloud amid economic uncertainty.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued headwinds from tough year-over-year comparisons through the remainder of 2022, particularly for advertising revenues. Foreign exchange is expected to create larger headwinds in Q3 due to dollar strengthening. The company will slow hiring pace with effects becoming more apparent in 2023, while continuing to invest in critical engineering and technical roles. CapEx will increase in 2022 with focus on technical infrastructure, particularly servers.
features.risks: Economic uncertainty is creating advertiser pullbacks across YouTube and Network, with varying impacts by geography and vertical including supply chain and inventory issues. The strong dollar is creating increasing foreign exchange headwinds on both revenue and operating income. Tough year-over-year comparisons from 2021's strong recovery will continue to pressure advertising growth rates for the remainder of the year.
features.key_drivers: Search revenue growth was driven by strong performance in travel and retail categories, with travel searches up significantly year-over-year. Google Cloud momentum continues across all geographies with growth in both GCP infrastructure/platform services and Workspace seats/ARPU. YouTube Shorts reached 1.5B monthly logged-in users with early monetization testing showing encouraging results. AI investments are driving product improvements across Search, YouTube, and Cloud with new features like multisearch and Performance Max gaining advertiser adoption.
